what_it_does: A standalone HTML preview card specimen for the display typeface (Sora 700): renders a 76px hero line with one word in gold accent, plus a metadata strip stating the font, weight, tracking, and usage.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation: group='Brand', name='Display — Sora 700', subtitle='Hero specimen with gold accent', viewport='700x240'.
Specimen line: 'The mathematics of operation.' with 'operation' wrapped in .a (gold accent).
Type set at 700 weight, 76px, line-height 0.98, letter-spacing -0.025em.
Meta strip: 'Sora', 'Display · 700', 'Tracking −2.5%', 'Used for: hero, screen titles'.
standouts: Specimen copy 'The mathematics of operation' reflects ConceptV's framing rather than placeholder text.
Demonstrates the gold-accent-on-one-word display pattern inline.
conventions: Follows the @dsCard gallery convention and var()-token discipline (font + colour tokens).
Hardcodes the display size/tracking (76px, -0.025em) in local CSS as specimen values rather than via scale tokens.
notes: Sora 700 display-type specimen card; a 76px hero line with a gold accent word and metadata declaring tracking -2.5% and hero/title usage.
questions: Is the -2.5% tracking value defined as a token applied to real display headings, or only shown here?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the @dsCard names it the Sora 700 display specimen and that is what renders.
intention_signals: Intended as a recognisable hero-type specimen so the display typeface, tracking, and gold-accent treatment can be judged by eye.
novelty: The gold-accented single word as a demonstrated display pattern, paired with brand-flavoured specimen copy.
contribution: Contributes the canonical display-typeface specimen (Sora 700) with its tracking value, gold-accent treatment, and hero/screen-title usage role.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML @dsCard preview specimen for Sora 700 as the design system's display typeface, showing a 76px hero line with a gold accent word and tracking/usage metadata.
intention_for_embedding: To give an eye-recognisable reference for the display typeface, its tracking, gold-accent treatment, and hero/title role.
